topic: Wine service temperature, glass and protocol

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A wine is prepared for service with a chosen temperature, suitable clean glassware and an orderly presentation procedure.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Temperature changes aroma and structure perception, glass shape changes aroma delivery, and protocol reduces handling errors and confirms the intended bottle.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can be presented consistently and assessed under conditions appropriate to its style.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: correct service combines temperature, glassware and presentation because each controls a different part of the experience.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
